codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers all expected behavior: organized filter grid, semantic action badges, JSON payload viewer with collapse/expand, IP/User-Agent metadata display, and pagination. One minor React key warning on a Fragment in the table map. No blocking issues.

issues.description: React key prop missing on the Fragment (<>) that wraps each Table.Tr pair in the map loop. Only the inner <Table.Tr> has a key. React requires all siblings in a map to have a key, including Fragment wrappers. While React 19 may not crash, this can cause console warnings and unpredictable reconciliation in edge cases. Fix: use <Fragment key={entry.id}> instead of <>.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The FilterBar component uses inline Tailwind utility classes (e.g., h-8 w-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-red-50 ring-1 ring-red-100) for the filter icon container, while the rest of the admin UI consistently uses the adminUi design token system. This creates a minor inconsistency with the design system established in -admin-ui.ts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FilterBar function at line 72 uses Box with className="flex h-8 w-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-red-50 ring-1 ring-red-100" instead of adminUi tokens

summary: The admin-auditoria-redesign feature passes review. All 6 expected behaviors are implemented correctly: filter grid with 5 filters (entityType, action, actor, dateFrom, dateTo), table with all required columns, semantic action badges (create=green, update=blue, remove=red, confirm=teal, cancel=orange, etc.), JSON payload viewer with collapse/expand, IP/User-Agent metadata in expanded rows, and pagination with prev/next and counter. One non-blocking React key warning exists on a Fragment in the table map. The FilterBar deviates slightly from the adminUi design token system by using inline Tailwind classes, but this is cosmetic and does not affect functionality.
